item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I gotta say, the Three Moons Initiative is by far my favorite Group of Interest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I suspect it would find a nice email-to-fax service.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Been reading into the Three Moons Initiative recently and I love this concept. Part of me wishes there was a system like this in real life, although I suppose it would be rather catastrophic even though there are situations today where "intervention" would be pretty nice.

I wonder what will happen if there was a situation where a targeted leader didn't have an email.

Regardless, +1

item.title: Re: A hostile tag?
item.content:encoded:
It is probably a remnant of the original ending, in which 2578 decided that the O5 Council were tyrants (it's not like there is a shortage of reasons for believing such…) and they all got a Sic Semper Tyrannis on their email inboxes at once. I like the "new" ending a bit better (well, it's not exactly new since the change was around two years ago) but I sort of miss the

spoiler
– hide block

"With all due respect, Naismith, I don't wanna fucking die." from the Overseer


in the original ending.

By the way, gotta get around to upvote now that I sort of reactivated my account. +1

item.title: A hostile tag?
item.content:encoded:
First of all, I love this skip to bits. +1

But why is it labeled as hostile? It's obviously hostile to SCP-2578-B instances, but it does not threaten the world at large, nor does it threaten the SCP foundation (aside from the accidental slaying of the SCP staff around 1427). Other notable SCPs tagged as hostile include Able for goodness' sake, and 4747, which is hostile to any humans that aren't the couple that performed the ritual; I don't think 2578 could be considered to have the same hostility.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I hope this isn't too egregious a necropost. I will accept any spankings meted out to me. :-/

I'm surprised I hadn't heard of this one sooner, I found out about it from a Facebook page. this had my upvote, and had me in tears, by the end of that message sent to the synagogue.

my first memory of the synagogue I attended — a synagogue that was a huge part of my life, filled with people who were like family to me, many of them survivors of the shoah — being desecrated, was when I was 8 years old. somebody splashed pigs blood all over our synagogue and left the pigs head perched on the handles of the doors to the temple. short of actual violence, that's about one of the most disgusting things you can do to a Jewish place of worship. it was horrible, and I just didn't understand. I didn't understand how or why anyone would hate us so much. I still don't understand. that much blind hatred still feels like a real waste of energy to me.

that they are saying "hey, we took care of that genocidal sicko for you. here's how you can pay us back for saving you from certain death. don't be afraid. you are safe, and you are loved." … well, yeah, it punched me right in the feels. hard.

I wish this one were real. maybe that makes me a monster. honestly, I'm ok with that. sic semper tyrannis.

i found it really engaging but my sentimentality may have lent to that. I'm looking forward to reading more about the triple moon.

thanks for this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think you mean "supreme crab protector."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A guardian angel with the universe's deadliest sniper rifle. Definitely worth a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The borehole was discovered through Mr. Lupescu's chest. Eyewitness accounts claimed that approximately five minutes before the Impaler event, Mr. Lupescu grew increasingly agitated for unknown reasons and insisted on letting his 4-year-old daughter ride on his shoulders.

Knowing that the Impaler event normally manifests as a vertical wound passing downward through the top of the head, this gave me chills. Nice touch!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm as much of a fan of skips which produce infinite evil bears or candy that turns kids into worms, but it does get a bit old after a while. It is wonderful to read about the Crab of Supreme Justice, which is ready and willing to impale monstrous but non-anomalous humans. Also, the bit about a spaceship with (presumably) a gigantic spike on the back causing "Impaler Events" was pretty awesome.

item.title: Re: Three Moons Got Three Digits!!!
item.content:encoded:
Honestly, I just used the term "fascist" loosely because I wanted to make a Woody Guthrie reference.

The title was originally "Remove Tyrant From Premises," but I ended up changing it after realizing the whole "Remove Kebab" meme was extremely insensitive about genocide.

item.title: Re: Three Moons Got Three Digits!!!
item.content:encoded:
This is two or three times now that I've seen the dictators targeted by 2578-D called "Fascists." Funny thing about that is that of the three targets detailed in the 2578-B list excerpt, the third one is fictional but probably a generic dictator, the first one is a generic dictator, and the second one is a royal in a monarchy. If you want to extend this to the target in China alluded to when 2578-D is discovered, that would also not be a fascist since China is, amazingly, one of the few communist nations that hasn't gone tits-up yet. Technically the skip didn't target a political leader when it broke the bronze stele in North Korea, but that was probably a preliminary obstacle in clearing the way to take out Kim Jong-Un, who again is a generic dictator once you take away the ludicrous god-cult his grandpa setup. And I'm still unsure who is implied by the latitude in the picture at the top of the article, but if that guy turns out to be an actual fascist, I'll eat my spare XBox controller.

Half of the trouble with the polarization of political views nowadays is people not being honest or precise with their speech.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Didn't the author of SCP-1427 state that he thought this was an acceptable way for it to be neutralized?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Came to the discussion to say the same. Why hasn't it been changed, if I may so ask?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, so it's officially canon that SCP-1427 was neutralized?

Nope. There is no canon.

item.title: Three Moons Got Three Digits!!!
item.content:encoded:
Okay, so it's officially canon that SCP-1427 was neutralized?
Why does it not say so on the official page for it?

Anyway, it's nice to know 🌙🌙🌙 is on our side, huh?
Honestly if I could, I would see to it being Heimel rather than Keter.
I know these Fascists it takes care of are not anomalous, that's why I know it won't slide.
But it does take care of such a problem for us as long as it's not behind bars…

Sorry, just some thoughts I have.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Got a few potential crosslinks to suggest for the badass space sniper. First off, the mention of MTF Iota-10 can be linked to the group's MTF page entry. Secondly, SCP-2578 could be a potential neutralization means for political SCPs that may get out of hand, including SCP-1679, and so it would not be so out of line for the Foundation to look into potential means of triggering it should such action be necessary. Finally, there is mention of SCP-2578 being used on SCP-682 in the Test Log, which itself is a major wikiwalk hub, and linking there would be both relevant and helpful to the wikiwalk as a whole. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

Global email networks are to be monitored by the Foundation for indicators of SCP-2578-A. If the counter on a confirmed instance of SCP-2578-A reaches 24, a response team from MTF Iota-10 ("Damn Feds") is to be dispatched to the location of the corresponding instance of SCP-2578-B in no more than 10 hours. Over the last two hours, the team will monitor the situation and await further instructions.

To this:

Global email networks are to be monitored by the Foundation for indicators of SCP-2578-A. If the counter on a confirmed instance of SCP-2578-A reaches 24, a response team from MTF Iota-10 ("Damn Feds") is to be dispatched to the location of the corresponding instance of SCP-2578-B in no more than 10 hours. Over the last two hours, the team will monitor the situation and await further instructions.

Changing this:

Two hours after the above message was received by O5 command, SCP-1427 was declared neutralized.

To this:

Two hours after the above message was received by O5 command, SCP-1427 was declared neutralized. Research into use of SCP-2578 as a means of neutralizing difficult to contain SCP objects, or SCP objects of political origin, is pending O5 approval.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Add this to the list of skips I wish were real. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The bronze stele snaps in two…

just kidding!

I feel like 2578-D is a bit of a Mary Sue-esque archetype (considering the single-handed liberation of a country with decades of indentured servitude, totalitarian control, and generally unpleasant things), but hopefully this SCP's history is fleshed out a bit more in the future. I still like the idea of some kind of otherworldly being doing dirty work to keep the world in a controlled state (within its dictation, but with unknown end-game motives).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the "230 aborted Impaler Events [in Saudi Arabia]" line.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, 2578-D seems friendly as long as you're not and aren't planning to become an evil dictator. If the Foundation could remove the stick from its collective ass enough to negotiate, -D could become a valuable ally. It already took out 1427 and did its best on 682. I know it's not popular to try to destroy even extremely dangerous Keter items anymore, but there are still times when putting a big hole through something or someone would solve a lot of problems.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this. Great idea and well written also the message has always been dear to me. Sic semper!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Better ending means I feel more confident about upvoting it.

item.title: Edit 5/22/17
item.content:encoded:
The ending was weak. I went with the better one.

(also, changed the title)

item.title: Re: SMASH THE FASH
item.content:encoded: …you know what? You're right. Update forthcoming.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this thing rather disproportionately. +1, and then I downvoted so I could upvote again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Even so, their current plight is better than life as it had been only a day prior. I thank you for your assistance, friend.

This is better than the ending in the article.

item.title: Re: SMASH THE FASH
item.content:encoded: If you don't mind me asking, why isn't that in the skip? I mean, you even have the times and everything thought out

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2578-B was relatively unopposed in these views by its constituents, and dissent or rebellion was curbed in such a way that, had the subject not died, all of its plans could have been successfully implemented.

In such a situation anyone who'd replace SCP-2578-B would likely share the same goals, and thus become a new SCP-2578-B, only to be assassinated and replaced, repeating the cycle. Such a string of successful assassinations in the same country would be very noticeable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I disagree with the final note being "silly". The scip found out that there's a clandestine organization who isn't merely complicit with existing dictatorships, but was ready to set up a new one in case the DPRK went belly-up and then needed a new population for containment. There's also the fact that O5 has been repeatedly shown to be made up of the most cruel sociopaths that the Foundation has ever produced with only the Ethics Oversight Committee being able to stop them from having free reign. So to me, it makes perfect sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This tower's status? Impaling fascists from Dallas to Malibu, check its resume, your Overseer's residue.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Any cow that's sacred gets defaced, like any tyrant murderer gets replaced.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I remember reading this first be envisioned in the chat, really cool to see how this played out!

+1, I really want to see this one in a tale

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, he's a former Romanian national player who was active in the 90s.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Emails? Politics? This seems a bit too political… oh well, still lots of mystery and anomaly. The three crescents were a symbol of the Ottoman Empire, which was a muslim Turkish republic that conquered the Byzantine Empire and threatened Christian Europe in the Balkans. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh? I didn't know there was a football player named that, I was trying to make a fictional Romanian name. :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not feeling this. "Hypnestics" feels like far too much convenient magic in the Foundation's hands, I dislike the idea of neutralizing one article with another, and the final note comes off as silly, almost lolFoundation. Too many experiments that don't pan out, in my opinion. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As the author of SCP-1427: this is a good use of that SCP, a plausible neutralization strategy, and the people responsible for containing it pretty much got what was coming to them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ioan Lupescu?

What's a football player doin' as a dictator?

item.title: Re: SMASH THE FASH
item.content:encoded:
As for this being the cause of the whole Broken Masquerade thing - I personally haven't read that canon, but if people want to accept this as the cause, I won't mind, and I'll include some specifics about what actually happened in incident 2578 under the collapsible.

+ show block
– hide block

17:15: Containment staff of SCP-1427 terminated in a simultaneous Impaler Event without warning. E-mails of apologies sent to the containment staff's families from SCP-2578-D.
17:29: SCP-2578-D sighted in the stratosphere over Pyongyang, descending rapidly from high earth orbit.
17:31: SCP-2578 infiltrates the containment area of SCP-1427 and begins direct bombardment with projected energy weapons on the ends of its "leg" attachments.
17:32: 1427 sustains heavy damage and snaps in two. SCP-2578-D begins to retreat, apparently leaving the stele for neutralized.
17:34: As an apparent defense mechanism, SCP-1427 begins to exhibit higher-than-usual output of electromagnetic energy, causing irreversible psychological damage to all those who were previously under its control.
17:36: SCP-2578-D returns, latches onto both halves of SCP-1427, and rapidly ascends into high Earth orbit.
18:12: Message from SCP-2578-D to Dr. Naismith:

"[three-crescent symbol] is victorious. Regrettably, I could not stop the stele from delivering its cowardly parting shot toward its slaves. Even so, their current plight is better than life as it had been only a day prior. I thank you for your assistance, friend.

"Also, I have sustained heavy external damage from expending this much energy in a short period of time, so if any tyrants come up while I'm in auto-repair for the next week, I'm afraid they'll have to wait a bit longer."

19:53: The two halves of SCP-1427 are seen by a Foundation satellite, drifting in the direction of the Sun.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What can I say, I'm a wrigleyville boy. <3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So I decided to see if the latitude in the image matched up with any real life locations…

Go Cubs, yeah?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Much better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Got me there. It's totally anonymous now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While I like the article, and I gave it an upvote, I do want to say that I'm not really a fan of the image caption. Mostly because I feel like it's pretty obvious who it's supposed to be under the black boxes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is, hands down, one of my new favorite SCPs. Fantastic work!

Enthusiastic +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You're on to… the gist of it.

That settles it, I'm posting in the Leak.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good to know! Correcting in a sec.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For a while, it felt as if I were talking to a wall in the back-and-forth with Mr. Jong-Un, but a bronze stele is close enough. It seems a more direct approach is needed for liberation.

FYI, Korean names are usually styled [SURNAME]1 followed by [GIVEN NAME].2 It is technically plausible to say "Mr. Jong-Un", but "Mr. Kim" would have been the default.

Footnotes
1. Usually one character, although some can be two characters (usually implying foreign descent).
2. Usually one or two characters.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So I'm guessing this thing came from Corbenic, given the 🌙🌙🌙.

That raises some serious questions! Why does this thing care about killing fascist leaders if it originates from the afterlife? Does it want to send fascists back to its home plane? Is it stuck on our plane? If I had to guess I'd think it was some sort or "judge" entity that either tortures fascists in Corbenic or alternatively, kills them in such a way that prevents them from reaching the afterlife at all.

This one is gonna leave me guessing.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: that message from O5-2 was +∞

item.title: Re: SMASH THE FASH
item.content:encoded: The three crescents do actually represent something else in one of my skips that hints at the craft's origin, but it's a secret, one that I'm debating putting in the "leaks" thread if this stays up. Or, I could tell you in private.

item.title: SMASH THE FASH
item.content:encoded:
What'd I tell ya? Fascists die, you don't fuck it up horribly, you get a +1.

I assume this implicitly ties into the Broken Masquerade canon? North Korea going catatonic would seem to account for the event described therein, if I remember correctly.

There's gotta be a more elegant way to do things than repeating [three-crescent symbol] over and over though. Does 🌙🌙🌙 reliably render on other people's machines?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love good scips, and I love the assassination of war criminals. +1
